result:
## Round 29 Goal: **2D Spatial Fourier Spectrum Analyzer**

Every analytical tool built so far (entropy, temperature, Lyapunov sensitivity) operates in **real space**. This round shifts to **frequency space** — a fundamentally different lens on the same automaton.

**The feature:** A real-time 2D discrete Fourier transform of the grid that reveals dominant spatial frequencies, periodic wavelengths, and structural regularity. Toggled with `F`, it will display:

1. **Power spectrum panel** — a compact log-scaled 2D spectral density plot (low frequencies center, high at edges) rendered with Unicode block shading
2. **Radial power profile** — a 1D plot of azimuthally-averaged power vs. spatial frequency (wavelength), showing whether the pattern is dominated by fine structure, coarse structure, or is scale-free
3. **Dominant wavelength indicator** — the peak spatial period (in cells) displayed on the HUD
4. **Spectral entropy** — a single scalar measuring how spread the energy is across frequencies (flat = noise, peaked = crystalline order)

**Why this matters at round 29:** Gliders have a characteristic wavelength. Oscillators appear as spectral peaks. Random soup is white noise. The phase transition at critical temperature (~0.15) should show a dramatic shift from peaked to broadband spectrum — connecting temperature (round 27) and entropy (round 26) through an entirely orthogonal analysis. This is the geometric Fourier complement to the information-theoretic tools already built.

**Implementation:** Pure C, no dependencies — a Cooley-Tukey radix-2 FFT on padded rows/columns (512×256), computed every ~8 generations when active.

Shall I build it?
